name: Bajan Gungo Pea and Coconut Soup-Barbados
recipeIngredient: 1/2 teaspoon minced habanero or jalapeno pepper (adjust to taste)
2 cups cooked or canned pigeon peas (gungo peas), drained
1 cup peeled, diced winter squash (Calabaza, butternut, or acorn)
1/2 teaspoon dried thyme or 2 tablespoons fresh thyme
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
2. Add the onion, carrot, celery, garlic, and chile pepper to the pan. Sauté for 7 minutes until the vegetables are tender.
3. Stir in the beans, water, squash, rum, parsley, thyme, black pepper, and salt.
4. Bring the mixture to a simmer, stirring occasionally, and cook for 35 to 40 minutes.
5. If the soup is too thick, add a little hot water or rum; if it's too thin, continue simmering until desired consistency is reached.
6. Stir in the coconut milk and return to a simmer briefly.
7. Transfer the soup to a food processor and process for 15 seconds until smooth.
8. Ladle the soup into bowls and serve hot. Serves 6-8.
Note: Calabaza (West Indian Pumpkin) can be found in tropical markets.
1 serving: calories 192 Sodium 177mg Fat 11g
